title: Rootless podman on EL7.8 can't start read-only containers if we have userns=keep-id
body:
I am not able to run containers with the read-only flag if I want to keep the id in my user-ns. Without keeping the id it works:

```
$ id -u
1011
$ podman  run -it  --read-only=true --user=root --userns=keep-id fedora:31                                                                                          
Error: container_linux.go:345: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:430: container init caused \"rootfs_linux.go:147: setting rootfs as readonly caused \\\"operation not permitted\\\"\"": OCI runtime permission denied error
$ podman  run -it  --read-only=true --user=root fedora:31
[root@0f44a3e2bb94 /]# 
```

Debug:

comments.body: This looks like it works on Fedora and perhaps newer versions of podman on RHEL8.  Any chance you could try this on RHEL8?
It works on Centos 8.1 using podman 1.6.4 - though this is not yet with cgroupv2
Please open a bugzilla on this, since this is not an issue in the upstream.
